title: Fleischmannia incarnata
taxonomy: Fleischmannia incarnata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Eupatorieae and is native to tropical regions. The plant is also known as the tropical false dragonhead and is native to parts of Central and South America. The classification of Fleischmannia incarnata reflects its close relation to other plants within the Eupatorieae tribe. This classification is used to understand the plant's evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Fleischmannia incarnata, a plant species, is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, Fleischmannia incarnata is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a possible treatment for certain infections. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in teas, infusions, or as topical applications for medicinal purposes. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Fleischmannia incarnata's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

constituents: Fleischmannia incarnata contains sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents.
